mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation, proxied by CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeding 3%, erodes corporate profit margins through higher input costs and signals restrictive future monetary policy, leading to negative equity returns as discount rates rise.
evidence_refs: fact:Recent inflation prints as of 2024-04-15 remain sticky above the Fed's 2% target.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 50% of triggered instances when CPI YoY is above 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in nonfarm payrolls growth (3-month moving average) indicates a cooling labor market and weakening aggregate demand, which negatively impacts expected corporate earnings and leads to lower forward equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Labor market strength has been a key support for the economy, so a slowdown is a significant signal.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in PAYEMS shows no significant negative correlation (p > 0.05) with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in both retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) over a 3-month period signals a broad-based contraction in consumer spending and investment, leading to downward revisions of GDP growth expectations and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Instances of joint 3-month declines in RSAFS and HOUST are not followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When real economic activity (INDPRO) is decelerating, an increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) significantly tightens financial conditions, raising the risk of a policy-induced recession and causing negative equity market performance.
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed has been holding rates high to combat inflation amid mixed growth signals.
failure_condition: In periods of negative INDPRO momentum, increases in FEDFUNDS are not followed by statistically significant negative 20-day returns compared to periods of positive INDPRO momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) from its 12-month low, specifically a rise of 0.5 percentage points or more, signals the onset of a recession (per Sahm's Rule) and triggers a significant equity market sell-off.
failure_condition: Trigger events are not followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 65% of historical cases since 1970.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects a robust supply side, strong corporate activity, and healthy earnings growth, which translates into positive forward equity returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically positive (p > 0.05) over the full sample.

mechanism_narrative: A strong upside surprise in the latest monthly retail sales (RSAFS) figure indicates resilient consumer demand, leading to upward revisions in near-term earnings estimates and positive short-term equity market returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending constitutes approximately 70% of US GDP.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns following a trigger event are not positive in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the real interest rate, proxied by the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) rising faster than inflation (CPIAUCSL YoY), tightens financial conditions, increases the discount rate for future earnings, and negatively impacts equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:Real rates are a key driver of asset allocation decisions.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 3-month change in the proxy for the real Fed Funds rate and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is not significantly negative.
